points.text: Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) — Hereditary Angioedema (HAE) — Phase 3 HAELO trial met primary endpoint with 87% attack reduction vs placebo (0.26 vs 2.10 monthly attacks, p<0.0001); rolling BLA submission initiated; U.S. launch anticipated H1 2027.
Intellia Therapeutics — Nex-z — ATTR-CM and ATTRv-PN — Resumed patient screening for Phase 3 MAGNITUDE and MAGNITUDE-2 trials; in vivo CRISPR gene editing candidate targeting TTR gene.
Intellia Therapeutics — Financial Position — Q1 2026 — Cash resources including April underwritten public offering proceeds expected to fund operations through at least 2028.

Washington University School of Medicine (WashU Medicine) — CRISPR-edited hookworms — Tetrodotoxin neutralization — Bioengineered hookworms successfully secreted neutralizing antibodies into hamster bloodstream, demonstrating a proof-of-concept for transgenic parasite-based drug delivery systems.

Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 HAELO trial: 62% of patients achieved attack-free and therapy-free status vs 11% placebo; rolling BLA submitted for one-time in vivo CRISPR treatment inactivating KLKB1 gene.
Intellia Therapeutics — Nex-z — ATTR-CM/ATTRv-PN — Phase 3 MAGNITUDE and MAGNITUDE-2 trials: Patient screening resumed for in vivo CRISPR candidate targeting transthyretin (TTR) gene.

Makedonka Mitreva / WashU Medicine — CRISPR-edited hookworm platform — Chronic disease/toxin exposure delivery — Published in Nature Communications; engineered worms produce toxin-neutralizing antibodies against tetrodotoxin, leveraging natural secretion mechanisms for long-term therapeutic delivery.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing commercialization — Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) + Nex-z pipeline — Phase 3 HAELO data positive; rolling BLA submitted; Q1 2026 cash runway extended to 2028 via public offering.

Washington University School of Medicine (WashU Medicine) — Transgenic parasite platform development — CRISPR-edited hookworms — Proof-of-concept established in hamsters for continuous antibody delivery; no commercial entity or deal value reported in source.

Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing inactivating KLKB1 gene to lower kallikrein/bradykinin — Phase: 3 (HAELO) — Population: Hereditary Angioedema — Comparator: Placebo — Results: 87% attack reduction (0.26 vs 2.10/month, p<0.0001); 62% attack-free/therapy-free vs 11% placebo.
Nex-z — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing targeting TTR gene — Phase: 3 (MAGNITUDE/MAGNITUDE-2) — Population: ATTR-CM and ATTRv-PN — Status: Patient screening resumed after prior pause.

CRISPR-edited hookworms — Mechanism: Genetic modification to secrete therapeutic antibodies — Phase: Preclinical (Hamster model) — Population: Hamsters exposed to tetrodotoxin — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Successful secretion of neutralizing antibodies into host bloodstream; no safety data or human trial status reported.
Intellia Therapeutics — Capital raise — Underwritten public offering in April 2026 — Proceeds combined with existing cash to fund operations through at least 2028.
